questionText: Show annual revenue, total cost, gross profit, gross margin percentage, and total order lines by fiscal year. Return columns: Fiscal Year, total_revenue, total_cost, gross_profit, gross_margin_pct, total_order_lines. Sort by Fiscal Year ascending.
questionLabel: [Q1][medium] Show annual revenue, total cost, gross profit, gross margin percentage, and total order lines by fiscal year. Return columns: Fiscal Year, total_revenue, total_cost, gross_profit, gross_margin_pct, total_order_lines. Sort by Fiscal Year ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    "Fiscal Year",
    SUM("Sales Amount") AS total_revenue,
    SUM("Total Product Cost") AS total_cost,
    SUM("Sales Amount") - SUM("Total Product Cost") AS gross_profit,
    (SUM("Sales Amount") - SUM("Total Product Cost")) / SUM("Sales Amount") AS gross_margin_pct,
    COUNT(*) AS total_order_lines
FROM "Sales"
JOIN "Date" ON "Sales"."OrderDateKey" = "Date"."DateKey"
GROUP BY "Fiscal Year"
ORDER BY "Fiscal Year" ASC;"}

questionText: Compare the Reseller and Internet sales channels showing total revenue, revenue share percentage, number of distinct sales orders, order lines, average line amount, and gross margin percentage. Return columns: Channel, total_revenue, revenue_share_pct, total_orders, order_lines, avg_line_amount, gross_margin_pct. Sort by total_revenue descending.
questionLabel: [Q2][hard] Compare the Reseller and Internet sales channels showing total revenue, revenue share percentage, number of distinct sales orders, order lines, average line amount, and gross margin percentage. Return columns: Channel, total_revenue, revenue_share_pct, total_orders, order_lines, avg_line_amount, gross_margin_pct. Sort by total_revenue descending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so.Channel,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS total_revenue,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(s."Sales Amount") / SUM(SUM(s."Sales Amount")) OVER (), 2) AS revenue_share_pct,
    COUNT(DISTINCT so."Sales Order") AS total_orders,
    COUNT(*) AS order_lines,
    ROUND(AVG(s."Sales Amount"), 2) AS avg_line_amount,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") / SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 2) AS gross_margin_pct
FROM 
    "Sales" s
JOIN 
    "Sales_Order" so ON s.SalesOrderLineKey = so.SalesOrderLineKey
GROUP BY 
    so.Channel
ORDER BY 
    total_revenue DESC;"}

questionText: Show revenue, revenue share percentage, units sold, gross profit, and gross margin percentage by product category. Return columns: Category, revenue, revenue_share_pct, units_sold, gross_profit, gross_margin_pct. Sort by revenue descending.
questionLabel: [Q3][hard] Show revenue, revenue share percentage, units sold, gross profit, and gross margin percentage by product category. Return columns: Category, revenue, revenue_share_pct, units_sold, gross_profit, gross_margin_pct. Sort by revenue descending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    p."Category",
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS revenue,
    ROUND(SUM(s."Sales Amount") * 100.0 / SUM(SUM(s."Sales Amount")) OVER (), 2) AS revenue_share_pct,
    SUM(s."Order Quantity") AS units_sold,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") AS gross_profit,
    ROUND(SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") * 100.0 / SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 2) AS gross_margin_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
GROUP BY p."Category"
ORDER BY revenue DESC"}

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    p."Product" AS "Product",
    p."Category" AS "Category",
    p."Subcategory" AS "Subcategory",
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS "revenue",
    SUM(s."Order Quantity") AS "units_sold",

JOIN

GROUP BY 
    p."Product", p."Category", p."Subcategory"
ORDER BY

LIMIT 10;"}

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    st."Group" AS "Group",
    st."Country" AS "Country",
    st."Region" AS "Region",
    d."Fiscal Year" AS "Fiscal Year",
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS revenue,
    SUM(s."Order Quantity") AS units_sold

    "Sales" s
JOIN 
    "Sales_Territory" st ON s."SalesTerritoryKey" = st."SalesTerritoryKey"

    "Date" d ON s."OrderDateKey" = d."DateKey"
GROUP BY 
    st."Group", st."Country", st."Region", d."Fiscal Year"
ORDER BY 
    st."Group" ASC, st."Country" ASC, st."Region" ASC, d."Fiscal Year" ASC;"}

questionText: List the top 15 direct customers (excluding CustomerKey -1) by lifetime revenue, showing customer name, city, state/province, country/region, total orders, total line items, total units, lifetime revenue, and lifetime profit. Return columns: Customer, City, State-Province, Country-Region, total_orders, total_line_items, total_units, lifetime_revenue, lifetime_profit. Sort by lifetime_revenue descending, limit to 15 rows.
questionLabel: [Q6][hard] List the top 15 direct customers (excluding CustomerKey -1) by lifetime revenue, showing customer name, city, state/province, country/region, total orders, total line items, total units, lifetime revenue, and lifetime profit. Return columns: Customer, City, State-Province, Country-Region, total_orders, total_line_items, total_units, lifetime_revenue, lifetime_profit. Sort by lifetime_revenue descending, limit to 15 rows.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    c."Customer",
    c."City",
    c."State-Province",
    c."Country-Region",
    COUNT(DISTINCT so."Sales Order") AS total_orders,
    COUNT(*) AS total_line_items,
    SUM(s."Order Quantity") AS total_units,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS lifetime_revenue,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") AS lifetime_profit
FROM 
    "Sales" s
JOIN 
    "Customer" c ON s."CustomerKey" = c."CustomerKey"
JOIN 
    "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
WHERE 
    s."CustomerKey" != -1
GROUP BY 
    c."Customer",
    c."City",
    c."State-Province",
    c."Country-Region"
ORDER BY 
    lifetime_revenue DESC
LIMIT 15"}

questionText: For the Reseller channel only, show reseller count, total revenue, revenue per reseller, gross profit, and margin percentage by business type. Return columns: Business Type, reseller_count, total_revenue, revenue_per_reseller, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by total_revenue descending.
questionLabel: [Q7][hard] For the Reseller channel only, show reseller count, total revenue, revenue per reseller, gross profit, and margin percentage by business type. Return columns: Business Type, reseller_count, total_revenue, revenue_per_reseller, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by total_revenue descending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    r."Business Type" AS "Business Type",
    COUNT(DISTINCT r."ResellerKey") AS reseller_count,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS total_revenue,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") / COUNT(DISTINCT r."ResellerKey") AS revenue_per_reseller,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") AS gross_profit,
    (SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") / SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100 AS margin_pct
FROM 
    "Sales" s
JOIN 
    "Reseller" r ON s."ResellerKey" = r."ResellerKey"
JOIN 
    "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
WHERE 
    so."Channel" = 'Reseller'
GROUP BY 
    r."Business Type"
ORDER BY 
    total_revenue DESC;"}

For the Reseller channel only, show reseller count, total revenue, revenue per reseller, gross profit, and margin percentage by business type. Return columns: Business Type, reseller_count, total_revenue, revenue_per_reseller, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by total_revenue descending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    r."Business Type" AS "Business Type",
    COUNT(DISTINCT r."ResellerKey") AS reseller_count,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS total_revenue,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") / COUNT(DISTINCT r."ResellerKey") AS revenue_per_reseller,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") AS gross_profit,
    (SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") / SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100 AS margin_pct
FROM 
    "Sales" s
JOIN 
    "Reseller" r ON s."ResellerKey" = r."ResellerKey"
JOIN 
    "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
WHERE 
    so."Channel" = 'Reseller'
GROUP BY 
    r."Business Type"
ORDER BY 
    total_revenue DESC;"}

questionLabel: [Q8][hard] Show monthly revenue, number of distinct sales orders, and units sold for each month. Return columns: Month, MonthKey, monthly_revenue, orders, units_sold. Sort by MonthKey ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    d."Month" AS Month,
    d."MonthKey" AS MonthKey,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS monthly_revenue,
    COUNT(DISTINCT so."Sales Order") AS orders,
    SUM(s."Order Quantity") AS units_sold
FROM 
    "Sales" s
JOIN 
    "Date" d ON s."OrderDateKey" = d."DateKey"
JOIN 
    "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
GROUP BY 
    d."Month", d."MonthKey"
ORDER BY 
    d."MonthKey" ASC;"}

questionText: Show order lines, revenue, units sold, revenue per unit (total revenue ÷ total units sold), average list price per product in the subcategory, gross profit, and margin percentage for each product subcategory. Return columns: Subcategory, order_lines, revenue, units_sold, revenue_per_unit, avg_list_price, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by revenue descending.
questionLabel: [Q9][hard] Show order lines, revenue, units sold, revenue per unit (total revenue ÷ total units sold), average list price per product in the subcategory, gross profit, and margin percentage for each product subcategory. Return columns: Subcategory, order_lines, revenue, units_sold, revenue_per_unit, avg_list_price, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by revenue descending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    p."Subcategory",
    COUNT(*) AS order_lines,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS revenue,
    SUM(s."Order Quantity") AS units_sold,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") / SUM(s."Order Quantity") AS revenue_per_unit,
    AVG(p."List Price") AS avg_list_price,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") AS gross_profit,
    (SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") / SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100 AS margin_pct
FROM 
    "Sales" s
JOIN 
    "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
GROUP BY 
    p."Subcategory"
ORDER BY 
    revenue DESC;"}

questionText: Show year-over-year revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal years. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_year, prior_revenue, current_year, current_revenue, yoy_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_year all ascending.
questionLabel: [Q10][hard] Show year-over-year revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal years. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_year, prior_revenue, current_year, current_revenue, yoy_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_year all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS prior_year,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS prior_revenue,
    d_current."Fiscal Year" AS current_year,
    SUM(s2."Sales Amount") AS current_revenue,
    ROUND((SUM(s2."Sales Amount") - SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 0), 2) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
JOIN "Date" d_prior ON s."OrderDateKey" = d_prior."DateKey"
JOIN "Sales" s2 ON s."ResellerKey" = s2."ResellerKey" 
    AND s."CustomerKey" = s2."CustomerKey" 
    AND s."ProductKey" = s2."ProductKey"
    AND s."SalesTerritoryKey" = s2."SalesTerritoryKey"
JOIN "Date" d_current ON s2."OrderDateKey" = d_current."DateKey"
    AND d_current."Fiscal Year" = CAST(CAST(d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS INTEGER) + 1 AS VARCHAR)
GROUP BY 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year",
    d_current."Fiscal Year"
ORDER BY 
    so."Channel" ASC,
    p."Category" ASC,
    prior_year ASC;"}

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS prior_year,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS prior_revenue,
    d_current."Fiscal Year" AS current_year,
    SUM(s2."Sales Amount") AS current_revenue,
    ROUND((SUM(s2."Sales Amount") - SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 0), 2) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
JOIN "Date" d_prior ON s."OrderDateKey" = d_prior."DateKey"
JOIN "Sales" s2 ON s."ResellerKey" = s2."ResellerKey" 
    AND s."CustomerKey" = s2."CustomerKey" 
    AND s."ProductKey" = s2."ProductKey"
    AND s."SalesTerritoryKey" = s2."SalesTerritoryKey"
JOIN "Date" d_current ON s2."OrderDateKey" = d_current."DateKey"
    AND d_current."Fiscal Year" = CAST(CAST(d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS INTEGER) + 1 AS VARCHAR)
GROUP BY

    d_current."Fiscal Year"
ORDER BY 
    so."Channel" ASC,
    p."Category" ASC,
    prior_year ASC;"}

Table "Date":
  Columns: DateKey (BIGINT), Date (BIGINT), Fiscal Year (VARCHAR), Fiscal Quarter (VARCHAR), Month (VARCHAR), MonthKey (BIGINT), Full Date (VARCHAR)

Table "Product":
  Columns: ProductKey (BIGINT), Product (VARCHAR), Standard Cost (DOUBLE), Color (VARCHAR), List Price (DOUBLE), Model (VARCHAR), Subcategory (VARCHAR), Category (VARCHAR), SKU (VARCHAR)

Table "Reseller":
  Columns: ResellerKey (BIGINT), Business Type (VARCHAR), Reseller (VARCHAR), City (VARCHAR), State-Province (VARCHAR), Country-Region (VARCHAR), Postal Code (VARCHAR), Reseller ID (VARCHAR)

Table "Sales":
  Columns: SalesOrderLineKey (BIGINT), ResellerKey (BIGINT), CustomerKey (BIGINT), ProductKey (BIGINT), OrderDateKey (BIGINT), DueDateKey (BIGINT), ShipDateKey (BIGINT), SalesTerritoryKey (BIGINT), Order Quantity (BIGINT), Unit Price (DOUBLE), Extended Amount (DOUBLE), Product Standard Cost (DOUBLE), Total Product Cost (DOUBLE), Sales Amount (DOUBLE), Unit Price Discount Pct (BIGINT)

Table "Sales_Order":
  Columns: SalesOrderLineKey (BIGINT), Sales Order (VARCHAR), Sales Order Line (VARCHAR), Channel (VARCHAR)

Table "Sales_Territory":
  Columns: SalesTerritoryKey (BIGINT), Region (VARCHAR), Country (VARCHAR), Group (VARCHAR)

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS prior_year,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS prior_revenue,
    d_current."Fiscal Year" AS current_year,
    SUM(s2."Sales Amount") AS current_revenue,
    ROUND((SUM(s2."Sales Amount") - SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 0), 2) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
JOIN "Date" d_prior ON s."OrderDateKey" = d_prior."DateKey"
JOIN "Sales" s2 ON s."ResellerKey" = s2."ResellerKey"

    AND s."SalesTerritoryKey" = s2."SalesTerritoryKey"
JOIN "Date" d_current ON s2."OrderDateKey" = d_current."DateKey"
    AND d_current."Fiscal Year" = CAST(CAST(d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS INTEGER) + 1 AS VARCHAR)
GROUP BY 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year",
    d_current."Fiscal Year"
ORDER BY 
    so."Channel" ASC,
    p."Category" ASC,
    prior_year ASC;"}

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS prior_year,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS prior_revenue,
    d_current."Fiscal Year" AS current_year,
    SUM(s2."Sales Amount") AS current_revenue,
    ROUND((SUM(s2."Sales Amount") - SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 0), 2) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
JOIN "Date" d_prior ON s."OrderDateKey" = d_prior."DateKey"
JOIN "Sales" s2 ON s."ResellerKey" = s2."ResellerKey" 
    AND s."CustomerKey" = s2."CustomerKey" 
    AND s."ProductKey" = s2."ProductKey"
    AND s."SalesTerritoryKey" = s2."SalesTerritoryKey"
JOIN "Date" d_current ON s2."OrderDateKey" = d_current."DateKey"
    AND d_current."Fiscal Year" = CAST(CAST(d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS INTEGER) + 1 AS VARCHAR)
GROUP BY 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year",
    d_current."Fiscal Year"
ORDER BY 
    so."Channel" ASC,
    p."Category" ASC,
    prior_year ASC;"}

function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",

    d_current."Fiscal Year" AS current_year,

    ROUND((SUM(s2."Sales Amount") - SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 0), 2) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"

JOIN "Sales" s2 ON s."ResellerKey" = s2."ResellerKey"

ORDER BY 
    so."Channel" ASC,
    p."Category" ASC,
    prior_year ASC;"}

questionText: Show year-over-year revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal years. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_year, prior_revenue, current_year, current_revenue, yoy_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_year all ascending.
questionLabel: [Q10][hard] Show year-over-year revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal years. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_year, prior_revenue, current_year, current_revenue, yoy_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_year all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS prior_year,

    d_current."Fiscal Year" AS current_year,

    ROUND(100.0 * (SUM(s2."Sales Amount") - SUM(s."Sales Amount")) / NULLIF(SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 0), 2) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
JOIN "Date" d_prior ON s."OrderDateKey" = d_prior."DateKey"
JOIN "Sales" s2 ON s."ResellerKey" = s2."ResellerKey" 
    AND s."CustomerKey" = s2."CustomerKey" 
    AND s."ProductKey" = s2."ProductKey"
    AND s."SalesTerritoryKey" = s2."SalesTerritoryKey"
JOIN "Date" d_current ON s2."OrderDateKey" = d_current."DateKey"
    AND d_current."Fiscal Year" = 'FY' || (CAST(SUBSTR(d_prior."Fiscal Year", 3) AS INTEGER) + 1)
WHERE d_prior."Fiscal Year" = 'FY2019'
GROUP BY 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year",
    d_current."Fiscal Year"
HAVING SUM(s."Sales Amount") > 100
ORDER BY 
    so."Channel" ASC,

Show year-over-year revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal years. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_year, prior_revenue, current_year, current_revenue, yoy_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_year all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS prior_year,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS prior_revenue,
    d_current."Fiscal Year" AS current_year,
    SUM(s2."Sales Amount") AS current_revenue,
    ROUND((SUM(s2."Sales Amount") - SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 0), 2) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
JOIN "Date" d_prior ON s."OrderDateKey" = d_prior."DateKey"
JOIN "Sales" s2 ON s."ResellerKey" = s2."ResellerKey" 
    AND s."CustomerKey" = s2."CustomerKey" 
    AND s."ProductKey" = s2."ProductKey"
    AND s."SalesTerritoryKey" = s2."SalesTerritoryKey"
JOIN "Date" d_current ON s2."OrderDateKey" = d_current."DateKey"
    AND d_current."Fiscal Year" = CAST(CAST(d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS INTEGER) + 1 AS VARCHAR)
GROUP BY 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year",
    d_current."Fiscal Year"
ORDER BY 
    so."Channel" ASC,
    p."Category" ASC,
    prior_year ASC;"}

You are a SQL query generator for DuckDB.
Generate SQL queries that answer the user's questions using DuckDB SQL syntax.
Make sure you quote all field, column and table names.

Here is the database schema:

Available tables:

Table "Customer":
  Columns: CustomerKey (BIGINT), Customer ID (VARCHAR), Customer (VARCHAR), City (VARCHAR), State-Province (VARCHAR), Country-Region (VARCHAR), Postal Code (VARCHAR)

Table "Date":
  Columns: DateKey (BIGINT), Date (BIGINT), Fiscal Year (VARCHAR), Fiscal Quarter (VARCHAR), Month (VARCHAR), MonthKey (BIGINT), Full Date (VARCHAR)

Table "Product":
  Columns: ProductKey (BIGINT), Product (VARCHAR), Standard Cost (DOUBLE), Color (VARCHAR), List Price (DOUBLE), Model (VARCHAR), Subcategory (VARCHAR), Category (VARCHAR), SKU (VARCHAR)

Table "Reseller":
  Columns: ResellerKey (BIGINT), Business Type (VARCHAR), Reseller (VARCHAR), City (VARCHAR), State-Province (VARCHAR), Country-Region (VARCHAR), Postal Code (VARCHAR), Reseller ID (VARCHAR)

Table "Sales":
  Columns: SalesOrderLineKey (BIGINT), ResellerKey (BIGINT), CustomerKey (BIGINT), ProductKey (BIGINT), OrderDateKey (BIGINT), DueDateKey (BIGINT), ShipDateKey (BIGINT), SalesTerritoryKey (BIGINT), Order Quantity (BIGINT), Unit Price (DOUBLE), Extended Amount (DOUBLE), Product Standard Cost (DOUBLE), Total Product Cost (DOUBLE), Sales Amount (DOUBLE), Unit Price Discount Pct (BIGINT)

Table "Sales_Order":
  Columns: SalesOrderLineKey (BIGINT), Sales Order (VARCHAR), Sales Order Line (VARCHAR), Channel (VARCHAR)

Show year-over-year revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal years. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_year, prior_revenue, current_year, current_revenue, yoy_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_year all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS prior_year,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS prior_revenue,
    d_current."Fiscal Year" AS current_year,
    SUM(s2."Sales Amount") AS current_revenue,
    ROUND((SUM(s2."Sales Amount") - SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 0), 2) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
JOIN "Date" d_prior ON s."OrderDateKey" = d_prior."DateKey"
JOIN "Sales" s2 ON s."ResellerKey" = s2."ResellerKey" 
    AND s."CustomerKey" = s2."CustomerKey" 
    AND s."ProductKey" = s2."ProductKey"
    AND s."SalesTerritoryKey" = s2."SalesTerritoryKey"
JOIN "Date" d_current ON s2."OrderDateKey" = d_current."DateKey"
    AND d_current."Fiscal Year" = CAST(CAST(d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS INTEGER) + 1 AS VARCHAR)
GROUP BY 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year",
    d_current."Fiscal Year"
ORDER BY 
    so."Channel" ASC,
    p."Category" ASC,
    prior_year ASC;"}

You are a SQL query generator for DuckDB.
Generate SQL queries that answer the user's questions using DuckDB SQL syntax.
Make sure you quote all field, column and table names.
Think through the problem step by step before writing the query.

Here is the database schema:

Available tables:

Table "Customer":
  Columns: CustomerKey (BIGINT), Customer ID (VARCHAR), Customer (VARCHAR), City (VARCHAR), State-Province (VARCHAR), Country-Region (VARCHAR), Postal Code (VARCHAR)

Table "Date":
  Columns: DateKey (BIGINT), Date (BIGINT), Fiscal Year (VARCHAR), Fiscal Quarter (VARCHAR), Month (VARCHAR), MonthKey (BIGINT), Full Date (VARCHAR)

Table "Product":
  Columns: ProductKey (BIGINT), Product (VARCHAR), Standard Cost (DOUBLE), Color (VARCHAR), List Price (DOUBLE), Model (VARCHAR), Subcategory (VARCHAR), Category (VARCHAR), SKU (VARCHAR)

Table "Reseller":
  Columns: ResellerKey (BIGINT), Business Type (VARCHAR), Reseller (VARCHAR), City (VARCHAR), State-Province (VARCHAR), Country-Region (VARCHAR), Postal Code (VARCHAR), Reseller ID (VARCHAR)

Table "Sales":
  Columns: SalesOrderLineKey (BIGINT), ResellerKey (BIGINT), CustomerKey (BIGINT), ProductKey (BIGINT), OrderDateKey (BIGINT), DueDateKey (BIGINT), ShipDateKey (BIGINT), SalesTerritoryKey (BIGINT), Order Quantity (BIGINT), Unit Price (DOUBLE), Extended Amount (DOUBLE), Product Standard Cost (DOUBLE), Total Product Cost (DOUBLE), Sales Amount (DOUBLE), Unit Price Discount Pct (BIGINT)

Table "Sales_Order":
  Columns: SalesOrderLineKey (BIGINT), Sales Order (VARCHAR), Sales Order Line (VARCHAR), Channel (VARCHAR)

Table "Sales_Territory":
  Columns: SalesTerritoryKey (BIGINT), Region (VARCHAR), Country (VARCHAR), Group (VARCHAR)


You have these tools available: run_sql_query(sql) - Execute a SQL query against the DuckDB database.

Workflow:
- Call run_sql_query with your SQL query. Important: only ever pass SQL to run_sql_query.
- If the query returns an error, call run_sql_query with a corrected query.
- When results look correct, call results_ok.

Show year-over-year revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal years. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_year, prior_revenue, current_year, current_revenue, yoy_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_year all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS prior_year,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS prior_revenue,
    d_current."Fiscal Year" AS current_year,
    SUM(s2."Sales Amount") AS current_revenue,
    ROUND((SUM(s2."Sales Amount") - SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 0), 2) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
JOIN "Date" d_prior ON s."OrderDateKey" = d_prior."DateKey"
JOIN "Sales" s2 ON s."ResellerKey" = s2."ResellerKey" 
    AND s."CustomerKey" = s2."CustomerKey" 
    AND s."ProductKey" = s2."ProductKey"
    AND s."SalesTerritoryKey" = s2."SalesTerritoryKey"
JOIN "Date" d_current ON s2."OrderDateKey" = d_current."DateKey"
    AND d_current."Fiscal Year" = CAST(CAST(d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS INTEGER) + 1 AS VARCHAR)
GROUP BY 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year",
    d_current."Fiscal Year"
ORDER BY 
    so."Channel" ASC,
    p."Category" ASC,
    prior_year ASC;"}

{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS prior_year,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS prior_revenue,
    d_current."Fiscal Year" AS current_year,
    SUM(s2."Sales Amount") AS current_revenue,
    ROUND(100.0 * (SUM(s2."Sales Amount") - SUM(s."Sales Amount")) / NULLIF(SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 0), 2) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
JOIN "Date" d_prior ON s."OrderDateKey" = d_prior."DateKey"
JOIN "Sales" s2 ON s."ResellerKey" = s2."ResellerKey" 
    AND s."CustomerKey" = s2."CustomerKey" 
    AND s."ProductKey" = s2."ProductKey"
    AND s."SalesTerritoryKey" = s2."SalesTerritoryKey"
JOIN "Date" d_current ON s2."OrderDateKey" = d_current."DateKey"
    AND d_current."Fiscal Year" = 'FY' || (CAST(SUBSTR(d_prior."Fiscal Year", 3) AS INTEGER) + 1)
WHERE d_prior."Fiscal Year" = 'FY2019'
GROUP BY 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year",
    d_current."Fiscal Year"
ORDER BY 
    so."Channel" ASC,
    p."Category" ASC,
    prior_year ASC;"}

You are a SQL query generator for DuckDB.
Generate SQL queries that answer the user's questions using DuckDB SQL syntax.
Make sure you quote all field, column and table names.
Think through the problem step by step before writing the query.

Here is the database schema:

Available tables:

Table "Customer":
  Columns: CustomerKey (BIGINT), Customer ID (VARCHAR), Customer (VARCHAR), City (VARCHAR), State-Province (VARCHAR), Country-Region (VARCHAR), Postal Code (VARCHAR)

Table "Date":
  Columns: DateKey (BIGINT), Date (BIGINT), Fiscal Year (VARCHAR), Fiscal Quarter (VARCHAR), Month (VARCHAR), MonthKey (BIGINT), Full Date (VARCHAR)

Table "Product":
  Columns: ProductKey (BIGINT), Product (VARCHAR), Standard Cost (DOUBLE), Color (VARCHAR), List Price (DOUBLE), Model (VARCHAR), Subcategory (VARCHAR), Category (VARCHAR), SKU (VARCHAR)

Table "Reseller":
  Columns: ResellerKey (BIGINT), Business Type (VARCHAR), Reseller (VARCHAR), City (VARCHAR), State-Province (VARCHAR), Country-Region (VARCHAR), Postal Code (VARCHAR), Reseller ID (VARCHAR)

Table "Sales":
  Columns: SalesOrderLineKey (BIGINT), ResellerKey (BIGINT), CustomerKey (BIGINT), ProductKey (BIGINT), OrderDateKey (BIGINT), DueDateKey (BIGINT), ShipDateKey (BIGINT), SalesTerritoryKey (BIGINT), Order Quantity (BIGINT), Unit Price (DOUBLE), Extended Amount (DOUBLE), Product Standard Cost (DOUBLE), Total Product Cost (DOUBLE), Sales Amount (DOUBLE), Unit Price Discount Pct (BIGINT)

Table "Sales_Order":
  Columns: SalesOrderLineKey (BIGINT), Sales Order (VARCHAR), Sales Order Line (VARCHAR), Channel (VARCHAR)

Table "Sales_Territory":
  Columns: SalesTerritoryKey (BIGINT), Region (VARCHAR), Country (VARCHAR), Group (VARCHAR)


You have these tools available: run_sql_query(sql) - Execute a SQL query against the DuckDB database.

Workflow:
- Call run_sql_query with your SQL query. Important: only ever pass SQL to run_sql_query.
- If the query returns an error, call run_sql_query with a corrected query.
- When results look correct, call results_ok.

Show year-over-year revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal years. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_year, prior_revenue, current_year, current_revenue, yoy_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_year all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS prior_year,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS prior_revenue,
    d_current."Fiscal Year" AS current_year,
    SUM(s2."Sales Amount") AS current_revenue,
    ROUND((SUM(s2."Sales Amount") - SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(s."Sales Amount"), 0), 2) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
JOIN "Date" d_prior ON s."OrderDateKey" = d_prior."DateKey"
JOIN "Sales" s2 ON s."ResellerKey" = s2."ResellerKey" 
    AND s."CustomerKey" = s2."CustomerKey" 
    AND s."ProductKey" = s2."ProductKey"
    AND s."SalesTerritoryKey" = s2."SalesTerritoryKey"
JOIN "Date" d_current ON s2."OrderDateKey" = d_current."DateKey"
    AND d_current."Fiscal Year" = CAST(CAST(d_prior."Fiscal Year" AS INTEGER) + 1 AS VARCHAR)
GROUP BY 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year",
    d_current."Fiscal Year"
ORDER BY 
    so."Channel" ASC,
    p."Category" ASC,
    prior_year ASC;"}

JOIN "Date" d_current ON s2."OrderDateKey" = d_current."DateKey"
    AND d_current."Fiscal Year" = 'FY' || (CAST(SUBSTR(d_prior."Fiscal Year", 3) AS INTEGER) + 1)
WHERE d_prior."Fiscal Year" = 'FY2019'
GROUP BY 
    so."Channel",
    p."Category",
    d_prior."Fiscal Year",
    d_current."Fiscal Year"
HAVING SUM(s."Sales Amount") > 100
ORDER BY 
    so."Channel" ASC,
    p."Category" ASC,
    prior_year ASC;"}

questionText: Show total revenue, units sold, revenue per unit (total revenue ÷ total units sold), average list price per product in the category, realized discount percentage versus that average list price, gross profit, and margin percentage by sales channel and product category. Return columns: Channel, Category, total_revenue, units_sold, revenue_per_unit, avg_list_price, realized_discount_pct, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by Channel ascending, then total_revenue descending.
questionLabel: [Q21][hard] Show total revenue, units sold, revenue per unit (total revenue ÷ total units sold), average list price per product in the category, realized discount percentage versus that average list price, gross profit, and margin percentage by sales channel and product category. Return columns: Channel, Category, total_revenue, units_sold, revenue_per_unit, avg_list_price, realized_discount_pct, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by Channel ascending, then total_revenue descending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel" AS Channel,
    p."Category" AS Category,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS total_revenue,
    SUM(s."Order Quantity") AS units_sold,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") / SUM(s."Order Quantity") AS revenue_per_unit,
    AVG(p."List Price") AS avg_list_price,
    (AVG(p."List Price") - (SUM(s."Sales Amount") / SUM(s."Order Quantity"))) / AVG(p."List Price") * 100 AS realized_discount_pct,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") AS gross_profit,
    (SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") / SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100 AS margin_pct
FROM 
    "Sales" s
JOIN 
    "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN 
    "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
GROUP BY 
    so."Channel", p."Category"
ORDER BY 
    Channel ASC, total_revenue DESC;"}

Show total revenue, units sold, revenue per unit (total revenue ÷ total units sold), average list price per product in the category, realized discount percentage versus that average list price, gross profit, and margin percentage by sales channel and product category. Return columns: Channel, Category, total_revenue, units_sold, revenue_per_unit, avg_list_price, realized_discount_pct, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by Channel ascending, then total_revenue descending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel" AS Channel,
    p."Category" AS Category,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS total_revenue,
    SUM(s."Order Quantity") AS units_sold,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") / SUM(s."Order Quantity") AS revenue_per_unit,
    AVG(p."List Price") AS avg_list_price,
    (AVG(p."List Price") - (SUM(s."Sales Amount") / SUM(s."Order Quantity"))) / AVG(p."List Price") * 100 AS realized_discount_pct,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") AS gross_profit,
    (SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") / SUM(s."Sales Amount")) * 100 AS margin_pct
FROM 
    "Sales" s
JOIN 
    "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN 
    "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
GROUP BY 
    so."Channel", p."Category"
ORDER BY 
    Channel ASC, total_revenue DESC;"}

questionText: Show quarter-over-quarter revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal quarters. questionLabel: [Q22][hard] Show quarter-over-quarter revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal quarters. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_quarter, prior_revenue, current_quarter, current_revenue, qoq_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_quarter all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    so."Channel" AS Channel,
    p."Category" AS Category,
    LAG(d."Fiscal Quarter") OVER (PARTITION BY so."Channel", p."Category" ORDER BY d."Fiscal Quarter") AS prior_quarter,
    LAG(SUM(s."Sales Amount")) OVER (PARTITION BY so."Channel", p."Category" ORDER BY d."Fiscal Quarter") AS prior_revenue,
    d."Fiscal Quarter" AS current_quarter,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS current_revenue,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(s."Sales Amount") - LAG(SUM(s."Sales Amount")) OVER (PARTITION BY so."Channel", p."Category" ORDER BY d."Fiscal Quarter")) 
        / NULLIF(LAG(SUM(s."Sales Amount")) OVER (PARTITION BY so."Channel", p."Category" ORDER BY d."Fiscal Quarter"), 0) * 100, 
        2
    ) AS qoq_growth_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
JOIN "Date" d ON s."OrderDateKey" = d."DateKey"
GROUP BY so."Channel", p."Category", d."Fiscal Quarter"
HAVING prior_quarter IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY Channel ASC, Category ASC, prior_quarter ASC;"}

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"WITH quarterly_revenue AS (
    SELECT 
        so."Channel" AS Channel,
        p."Category" AS Category,
        d."Fiscal Quarter" AS fiscal_quarter,
        SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS revenue
    FROM "Sales" s
    JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
    JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
    JOIN "Date" d ON s."OrderDateKey" = d."DateKey"
    GROUP BY so."Channel", p."Category", d."Fiscal Quarter"
)
SELECT 
    Channel,
    Category,
    LAG(fiscal_quarter) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY fiscal_quarter) AS prior_quarter,
    LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY fiscal_quarter) AS prior_revenue,
    fiscal_quarter AS current_quarter,
    revenue AS current_revenue,
    ROUND(
        (revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY fiscal_quarter)) 
        / NULLIF(LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY fiscal_quarter), 0) * 100, 
        2
    ) AS qoq_growth_pct
FROM quarterly_revenue
WHERE LAG(fiscal_quarter) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY fiscal_quarter) IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY Channel ASC, Category ASC, prior_quarter ASC;"}

{"sql":"WITH quarterly_revenue AS (
    SELECT 
        so."Channel" AS Channel,
        p."Category" AS Category,
        d."Fiscal Quarter" AS fiscal_quarter,
        SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS revenue
    FROM "Sales" s
    JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s."SalesOrderLineKey" = so."SalesOrderLineKey"
    JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
    JOIN "Date" d ON s."OrderDateKey" = d."DateKey"
    GROUP BY so."Channel", p."Category", d."Fiscal Quarter"
),
with_lag AS (
    SELECT 
        Channel,
        Category,
        LAG(fiscal_quarter) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY fiscal_quarter) AS prior_quarter,
        LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY fiscal_quarter) AS prior_revenue,
        fiscal_quarter AS current_quarter,
        revenue AS current_revenue,
        ROUND(
            (revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY fiscal_quarter)) 
            / NULLIF(LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY fiscal_quarter), 0) * 100, 
            2
        ) AS qoq_growth_pct
    FROM quarterly_revenue
)
SELECT 
    Channel,
    Category,
    prior_quarter,
    prior_revenue,
    current_quarter,
    current_revenue,
    qoq_growth_pct
FROM with_lag
WHERE prior_quarter IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY Channel ASC, Category ASC, prior_quarter ASC;"}

questionLabel: [Q23][hard] For each product category, list the top 2 subcategories by revenue, showing revenue rank within the category, revenue, units sold, gross profit, and revenue share percentage within the category. Return columns: Category, revenue_rank, Subcategory, revenue, units_sold, gross_profit, category_revenue_share_pct. Sort by Category, revenue_rank, then Subcategory all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    p."Category",
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY p."Category" ORDER BY SUM(s."Sales Amount") DESC) AS revenue_rank,
    p."Subcategory",
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS revenue,
    SUM(s."Order Quantity") AS units_sold,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") AS gross_profit,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(s."Sales Amount") / SUM(SUM(s."Sales Amount")) OVER (PARTITION BY p."Category"), 2) AS category_revenue_share_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Product" p ON s."ProductKey" = p."ProductKey"
GROUP BY p."Category", p."Subcategory"
HAVING revenue_rank <= 2
ORDER BY p."Category", revenue_rank, p."Subcategory" ASC;"}

questionText: Within each sales channel, show revenue, revenue share percentage, cumulative revenue share percentage ordered by revenue descending, number of distinct sales orders, and gross profit by product category. Return columns: Channel, Category, revenue, channel_revenue_share_pct, cumulative_channel_revenue_share_pct, orders, gross_profit. questionLabel: [Q24][hard] Within each sales channel, show revenue, revenue share percentage, cumulative revenue share percentage ordered by revenue descending, number of distinct sales orders, and gross profit by product category. Return columns: Channel, Category, revenue, channel_revenue_share_pct, cumulative_channel_revenue_share_pct, orders, gross_profit. Sort by Channel ascending, then revenue descending, then Category ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    s.Channel,
    p.Category,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS revenue,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(s."Sales Amount") / SUM(SUM(s."Sales Amount")) OVER (PARTITION BY s.Channel), 2) AS channel_revenue_share_pct,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(SUM(s."Sales Amount")) OVER (PARTITION BY s.Channel ORDER BY SUM(s."Sales Amount") DESC) / SUM(SUM(s."Sales Amount")) OVER (PARTITION BY s.Channel), 2) AS cumulative_channel_revenue_share_pct,
    COUNT(DISTINCT so."Sales Order") AS orders,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount" - s."Total Product Cost") AS gross_profit
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s.SalesOrderLineKey = so.SalesOrderLineKey
JOIN "Product" p ON s.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
GROUP BY s.Channel, p.Category
ORDER BY s.Channel ASC, revenue DESC, p.Category ASC"}

questionLabel: [Q25][hard] For each territory group and fiscal year, show the top sales channel by revenue, along with that channel's revenue, the total revenue for the group-year, and the channel's share of the group-year revenue. function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    st."Group" AS "Group",
    d."Fiscal Year" AS "Fiscal Year",
    so.Channel AS top_channel,
    SUM(s."Sales Amount") AS channel_revenue,
    SUM(SUM(s."Sales Amount")) OVER (PARTITION BY st."Group", d."Fiscal Year") AS group_year_revenue,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(s."Sales Amount") / SUM(SUM(s."Sales Amount")) OVER (PARTITION BY st."Group", d."Fiscal Year"), 2) AS channel_revenue_share_pct
FROM "Sales" s
JOIN "Sales_Territory" st ON s.SalesTerritoryKey = st.SalesTerritoryKey
JOIN "Date" d ON s.OrderDateKey = d.DateKey
JOIN "Sales_Order" so ON s.SalesOrderLineKey = so.SalesOrderLineKey
GROUP BY st."Group", d."Fiscal Year", so.Channel
QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY st."Group", d."Fiscal Year" ORDER BY SUM(s."Sales Amount") DESC) = 1
ORDER BY "Group", "Fiscal Year" ASC;"}
